recording_notes: Phone filming RCA monitor — close framing. Oblique angle limits exact wrap count and lead-hand identification.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument becomes visible; needle-suture complex brought into field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear task start; short pause before stable driving orientation.

frame_analyses.description: Needle reoriented near superior target for bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Several re-grasps to optimize angle and wrist orientation.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through Penrose; opposite instrument helping receive and pull through.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled tissue handling; bites close to marks.

frame_analyses.description: First knot throw over slit after suture drawn through.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot configuration with likely double wrap; angle limits certainty.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw formed with opposite instrument leading and countertraction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch present; slowed by strand organization and re-grasping.

frame_analyses.description: Switch back; third throw begins over existing knot stack.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternation present; corrective pulls prolong seating.

frame_analyses.description: Final tightening applied; knot seated over Penrose slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Flattens over slit without loss of purchase or drain injury.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting instrument introduced; suture tails positioned for trimming.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Least efficient phase; substantial time presenting tails.

frame_analyses.description: Both tails cut; field left with knot in place over slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion without avulsion or knot breakdown.

knot_assessments.notes: Likely double-wrapped before cinching; right-led, moderate confidence.
Opposite instrument leads; single throw seats after brief re-grasping pause.
Lead switches back to right; final seating takes extra corrective pulls, knot intact.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No obvious residual gap after knot tightened; final closure appears complete or near-complete.

technique_summary: Task completed without drain avulsion; bites landed close to marks. Knot sequence technically correct with likely surgeon's first throw and alternating lead hands on 2 and 3. Efficiency drops during strand management and especially the prolonged cutting phase. Final approximation appears secure without visible residual gap.
improvement_suggestions: Load needle into stable driving angle sooner to shorten setup and first bite.
Reduce re-grasps between throws by separating standing and free ends earlier.
Present both tails cleanly before introducing scissors to shorten cutting phase.
strengths: Maintained control of Penrose throughout with no avulsion or tissue injury.
Placement close to target marks despite oblique recording angle.
Alternating instrument leadership appears preserved; final knot stays seated.
comparison_notes: Near-exact time agreement with Claude V25 (Δ0.5s). FLS delta 6.0 driven by (a) drain-gap divergence — Claude flags gap_visible=true, GPT-4o sees complete closure, and (b) Claude applies out-of-rubric quality penalties (8.0 total vs GPT-4o's 2.5). This is the 6th drain-gap divergence case in the V-series.
